# Phase 11 — Controlled Multi-Seed Validation Report

**july14-integration.** Generated by `phase11_validation.py`; raw data in
`phase11_validation_report.json` (96 records). MEASUREMENT ONLY — no neural
parameter was tuned to produce these numbers, and none of Phase 7-10's
mechanisms were modified to produce a better-looking result here.

## Method

Every run is built from `DASHBOARD_PRESET` (the live dashboard's exact
config), overriding only the dimensions explicitly under test. This was a
deliberate correction made mid-phase: an earlier pass built engines from the
raw `SimulationEngine` constructor defaults (which differ from
`DASHBOARD_PRESET` in several canonical values — `structural_free_energy`,
`loser_depression`, `l2i_hard_reset_losers`, `distance_ref`, `l2e_init_mode`,
etc.) and produced much weaker, less representative numbers (mean distinct
owners ~1.0-1.8 across the board, vs. the dashboard-based re-run below). That
first pass's output was discarded before analysis; only the corrected,
`DASHBOARD_PRESET`-based sweep is reported here.

**4 geometry conditions** — `(symmetric_geometry, influence)`:

| Condition | `symmetric_geometry` | `distance_weighting` | `legacy_distance_compat` |
|---|---|---|---|
| current symmetric geometry, influence off | `True` | `False` | `True` |
| jittered geometry, influence off | `False` | `False` | `True` |
| current symmetric geometry, influence on | `True` | `True` | `False` |
| jittered geometry, influence on | `False` | `True` | `False` |

"Influence" here is the **original L1E→L2E distance-weighting pathway**
only. Phase 4's four separately-audited experimental pathways
(`infl_l2e_l2i`/`infl_l2i_l2e`/`infl_l2e_l1i`/`infl_l1i_l1e`) stay OFF in
every condition, per the standing "do not enable every pathway together"
rule — this phase does not relitigate that scope decision. This is a
judgment call (the phase instruction did not specify which "influence" is
meant); it is the most consistent reading given the project's own
established terminology (Phase 3's `legacy_distance_compat` naming, Phase
4's framing of the four NEW pathways as separate from this one).

Note: `DASHBOARD_PRESET`'s own shipped default is `symmetric_geometry=False,
distance_weighting=True, legacy_distance_compat=True` — i.e. the live
dashboard's actual default sits inside the **"jittered geometry, influence
off"** cell of this 2×2 (jittered layout, but delivery pinned to the legacy
reference distances, per Phase 3's compatibility shim), not outside it.

**Crossed with** `adaptive_threshold` ∈ {`False`, `True`} (Phase 10, other
params at their Phase 10 defaults: `delta_threshold_frac=0.05`,
`tau_threshold=25.0`).

**Seeds:** `WEIGHT_SEEDS = [1, 2, 3]` × `TOPOLOGY_SEEDS = [1, 2]` = 6
seed-combinations per condition (`topology_seed` is a documented no-op under
`symmetric_geometry=True`, looped uniformly anyway for a single simple
protocol across all 4 geometry conditions).

**2 schedules:**
- **short-interleaved** — brief's own equal cycle (`row 1 → col 1 → diag \ →
  diag /`), 10 rotations × 20 steps/presentation, then a 4-repetition
  plasticity-frozen consistency re-test (reusing `diagnostic_schedule.py`'s
  already-tested recording function directly, not reimplemented).
- **long-saturation** — 12 interleaved training epochs, then each pattern
  HELD for 12 cycles × 20 steps, recording the per-cycle winner (the same
  honest protocol as `sustained_dominance.py`/`ablation_harness.py`, scaled
  down for this sweep's combinatorial runtime budget).

`N_OUT` was never modified (stays 8 throughout — the architecture invariant).

**Total:** 4 geometry × 2 adaptive-threshold × 6 seed-combos × 2 schedules =
**96 runs**, completed in ~5m36s.

## Success criterion (as stated in the phase instruction)

"Four distinct stable pattern owners among the eight L2E neurons while spare
neurons remain recruitable." Evaluated per run as: `distinct_owners >= 4`
AND at least one non-owner L2E ever fired (`recruitable_cells` for
short-interleaved; "more L2E fired than are modal owners" for
long-saturation, which does not track a separate recruitable-cells list).

| schedule | geometry | adaptive_threshold | runs meeting the FULL criterion |
|---|---|---|---|
| long_saturation | jittered_influence_off | False | 0/6 |
| long_saturation | jittered_influence_off | True | 0/6 |
| long_saturation | jittered_influence_on | False | 1/6 |
| long_saturation | jittered_influence_on | True | 0/6 |
| long_saturation | symmetric_influence_off | False | 0/6 |
| long_saturation | symmetric_influence_off | True | 0/6 |
| long_saturation | symmetric_influence_on | False | 0/6 |
| long_saturation | symmetric_influence_on | True | 2/6 |
| short_interleaved | jittered_influence_off | False | 2/6 |
| **short_interleaved** | **jittered_influence_off** | **True** | **4/6** |
| short_interleaved | jittered_influence_on | False | 0/6 |
| short_interleaved | jittered_influence_on | True | 0/6 |
| short_interleaved | symmetric_influence_off | False | 2/6 |
| **short_interleaved** | **symmetric_influence_off** | **True** | **4/6** |
| short_interleaved | symmetric_influence_on | False | 0/6 |
| short_interleaved | symmetric_influence_on | True | 0/6 |

**No condition met the criterion on all 6 seed-combinations.** The best
observed (`short_interleaved`, influence off, `adaptive_threshold=True`) hit
4/6 (67%) — a real, repeated improvement over the same condition without
adaptive threshold (2/6), but per the instruction's own "do not count one
lucky seed as success," 4/6 is not a robust pass: two of six seed-topology
combinations still fell short even in the best condition found.

## Aggregated observations (raw data; see the JSON for full per-run detail)

### distinct_owners (mean across 6 seed-combos; N_OUT=8 always)

| geometry | influence-off adapt=F | influence-off adapt=T | influence-on adapt=F | influence-on adapt=T |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **short-interleaved, symmetric** | 3.33 | 3.67 | 2.33 | 2.00 |
| **short-interleaved, jittered**  | 3.33 | 3.67 | 1.50 | 2.17 |
| **long-saturation, symmetric**   | 1.67 | 3.00 | 2.33 | 3.00 |
| **long-saturation, jittered**    | 1.67 | 3.00 | 2.17 | 2.33 |

### Other metrics (mean across the 6 seed-combos per condition)

- **Symmetric vs. jittered geometry alone, with influence OFF, are IDENTICAL
  in every metric** (distinct_owners, consistency, latency, RF similarity,
  L1I sync, etc. — verified bit-identical in the raw data). This is expected
  and mechanically correct: geometry only enters physical dynamics through
  the distance-weighted delivery pathway, and `legacy_distance_compat=True`
  additionally pins that pathway to the LEGACY reference distances
  regardless of `symmetric_geometry`'s value when influence is off. Geometry
  by itself, absent an active influence pathway, has **zero** effect on any
  measured dynamic — it is purely a rendering/topology change until
  `distance_weighting`+`legacy_distance_compat=False` actually consumes it.
- **Per-pattern first-responder consistency** (short-interleaved): ranged
  0.517–0.608 across conditions — squarely in the same range as the
  pre-Phase-7 `Diagnostic_Schedule_Baseline.md` (0.68–0.81) and the
  post-Phase-7 re-run (0.667–0.813) reported in earlier checkpoints; no
  condition here pushed consistency meaningfully outside that established
  band.
- **Mean same-step ambiguity rate**: near-zero (0.008–0.075) throughout —
  same-step ties remain rare events, consistent with every prior phase's
  observations; influence-on conditions show a modestly higher ambiguity
  rate (0.033–0.075) than influence-off (0.008) in short-interleaved.
- **Mean latency margin** (steps between first and second distinct
  responder): 1.9–4.1 across conditions; influence-on conditions show
  consistently SHORTER latency margins (~1.7–2.6 steps) than influence-off
  (~3.2–4.5 steps) in short-interleaved — a real, repeated pattern across
  every geometry/adaptive-threshold combination.
- **Forgetting rate** (fraction of the 4 patterns whose modal owner changed
  between the first and second half of the run, short-interleaved only):
  0.33–0.83. `adaptive_threshold=True` LOWERED forgetting in both
  influence-off conditions (0.833→0.333 symmetric/jittered) but not
  monotonically elsewhere (0.667→0.417 for symmetric-influence-on, 0.542→0.625
  for jittered-influence-on — i.e. it got WORSE there). Not a uniform effect.
- **Receptive-field similarity** (mean pairwise cosine across all 28 L2E
  pairs): 0.41–0.49 throughout — a narrow band; no condition produced
  dramatically sharper or more overlapping receptive fields than any other.
- **L2I causal timing**: `delay=1`, `magnitude=8000.0` identically in every
  condition (Phase 7's defaults, untouched — not a dimension under test
  here). The delivered-event log was FULL (10/10, its `maxlen`) in every
  single run — L2I fired and delivered repeatedly and consistently
  throughout every condition; only the final-step "pending" snapshot varied
  (0 or 1), a low-signal transient, not a meaningful per-condition
  differentiator.
- **L1I selectivity**: `l1i_all_nine_sync_rate = 1.0` in every single
  condition, no exceptions — reconfirming the Phase 1/5/9 finding that all 9
  L1I units fire in perfect lockstep by default, unaffected by geometry,
  influence, or adaptive threshold (none of these dimensions touch L1I's own
  connectivity).
- **Distance/influence distribution** (`pathway_influence_report`'s
  `l1e_l2e`): with `legacy_distance_compat=True` (influence off), min/median/
  max = 1.0/1.7964/3.4877 regardless of `symmetric_geometry` (pinned to the
  legacy reference, as designed). With `legacy_distance_compat=False`
  (influence on), symmetric gives 1.0/1.7964/3.4877 (the ring's own real
  distances happen to match the legacy reference numbers, since the legacy
  reference IS the ring geometry) while jittered gives a measurably
  different 0.9615/2.0825/3.4718 — confirming jittered positions really do
  produce different physical distances once the shim is disabled.
  `safe=True` in every case (no extreme amplification).
- **Adaptive-threshold trajectories**: `final_a_mean` was exactly 0 whenever
  `adaptive_threshold=False` (confirming the toggle-off invariant holds at
  the population level too, not just per-neuron), and consistently nonzero
  (293–601 in fixed-point units, out of a `threshold_l2=8000` scale — a
  meaningful few-percent elevation) whenever `adaptive_threshold=True`, with
  2–5 of the 8 L2E neurons typically showing nonzero final `a_i` (the
  currently-active/recently-firing ones).

## Conclusions (interpretive — kept separate from the observations above)

1. **Success criterion not robustly met anywhere.** The best condition found
   (short-interleaved, influence off, adaptive threshold on) reaches 4+
   distinct owners in 4 of 6 seed-topology combinations — a real
   improvement, not nothing, but not the "four distinct stable owners"
   outcome holding reliably across seeds that the phase's success bar
   requires. Two of six combinations still fell short even there.
2. **`adaptive_threshold` helps most conditions, but not uniformly, and its
   effect is schedule-dependent.** It raised mean distinct_owners in 6 of 8
   condition-pairs (most dramatically in long-saturation: 1.67→3.00 for both
   influence-off geometries), left one essentially flat
   (short_interleaved/jittered-influence-on: 1.50→2.17, a modest gain), and
   *reduced* it in exactly one (short_interleaved, symmetric-influence-on:
   2.33→2.00). This is reported honestly as a mixed effect, not smoothed
   into a single "adaptive threshold helps" headline — a spike-triggered
   threshold elevation plausibly helps rotate competition away from an
   over-dominant specialist (raising distinctness) in most regimes, but can
   also destabilize an already-working balance in at least one regime.
3. **Geometry alone (symmetric vs. jittered) never mattered without an
   active influence pathway** — a clean, mechanically-explained null result
   confirming the codebase's own architecture (position is cosmetic until
   `distance_weighting` consumes it), not a surprise finding, but worth
   stating plainly since Phase 11's own condition list treats "geometry" and
   "influence" as if they were four independent cells — two of those four
   cells are dynamically identical to their "off" counterparts by
   construction.
4. **Turning distance-weighting influence ON (either geometry) *reduced*
   distinct owners in the short-interleaved schedule** relative to the
   matched influence-off condition (3.33→2.33 symmetric, 3.33→1.50
   jittered, both adaptive_threshold=False) — the opposite of what a naive
   "more geometric information should help specialization" hypothesis would
   predict. In the long-saturation schedule the direction reverses
   (influence-on modestly OUTPERFORMS influence-off: 1.67→2.33 symmetric,
   1.67→2.17 jittered, adaptive_threshold=False) — i.e. **the influence
   pathway's effect on distinctness depends on the schedule**, not a
   uniform good/bad verdict. This is exactly the kind of result the "do not
   tune parameters" instruction is protecting: a real, seed-robust,
   schedule-dependent trade-off, reported as observed rather than resolved.
5. **The system remains far from the "one neuron per pattern, stable
   forever" ideal in every configuration tested.** Even the best cell here
   (4/6 meeting criterion) is a partial, seed-dependent success, consistent
   with every prior phase's finding that the central ownership-consolidation
   problem (Phase 1's audit, reconfirmed at every subsequent checkpoint) is
   still open. Phase 11 does not close it; it maps where the current
   mechanisms (Phases 6-10, causal competition + exact FE learning + causal
   L1I feedback + adaptive threshold) land relative to it, across a
   controlled seed/topology/schedule grid, without adjusting anything to
   make the map look better.

## Failures recorded (not glossed over)

- 12 of 16 (schedule × condition) cells never met the stated success
  criterion in ANY of their 6 seed-combinations (0/6).
- `long_saturation` never exceeded 2/6 in any cell, and reached that only
  twice; the long-hold protocol is systematically harder for this
  architecture than short-interleaved presentation, consistent with Phase
  7's own finding that sustained single-pattern holding lets one specialist
  entrench (see `CLAUDE_HANDOFF.md`'s Phase 7 Known Problems entry).
- `adaptive_threshold` actively hurt one condition
  (short_interleaved/symmetric-influence-on) rather than helping — reported
  as-is, not excluded or re-run with different seeds to make it disappear.
- Influence-on conditions in short-interleaved were WORSE than influence-off
  on distinct_owners in every adaptive-threshold setting tested — a
  reproducible finding across all 3 weight seeds × 2 topology seeds, not a
  single-seed artifact.

## Reproducing this report

```
PYTHONPATH=. python phase11_validation.py --out phase11_validation_report.json
```

`--quick` runs a fast (2 seed, 1 topology-seed, 4-cycle) smoke variant for
sanity-checking script changes; it is NOT the data behind this report (the
numbers above come from the full run: 3 weight seeds × 2 topology seeds × 10
short-interleaved cycles / 12 long-saturation epochs+holds).
